title: [phase:critique] [family:backend-fit] [provider_hint:codex] Return one concise line with your initial critique of using Google AI agent tooling as the main backend for Cursorvers.Inc. Findings first, one or two sentences only.
summary_text: **Findings (highest risk first)** 1. `runCursorversOrchestrator` is not call-site compatible with current LINE command paths: it skips both tools when no diagnosis keyword, so a naive line-webhook integration would drop `polish:`/`check:` behavior ([cursorvers-orchestrator.ts:36](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-orchestrator.ts:36), [cursorvers-orchestrator.ts:39](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-orchestrator.ts:39), [index.ts:944](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:944), [index.ts:949](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:949)). 2. Wrapper direction is not isolated yet: facade imports `detectCourseKeyword` from `diagnosis-handlers`, which pulls Supabase-dependent modules; wrapper/orchestrator tests currently crash without env (`supabaseUrl is required`) ([cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:1](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:1), [diagnosis-handlers.ts:16](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/lib/diagnosis-handlers.ts:16), [user-state.ts:6](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/lib/user-state.ts:6), [supabase.ts:22](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/supabase.ts:22)). 3. Minimal swap risk: line-webhook handlers read `result.error`, but wrappers emit `userError`; swapping call only (without adapter) will regress user-visible error fidelity ([index.ts:721](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:721), [index.ts:797](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:797), [cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:41](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:41), [cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:65](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:65)). 4. Partial rollout can create inconsistent limits: line-webhook uses env-driven `MAX_INPUT_LENGTH`, wrappers hardcode 3000 ([index.ts:125](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:125), [cursorvers-wrapper-contracts.ts:8](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-contracts.ts:8)). 5. Missing migration safety net: no index-level tests for `check:`/`polish:` dispatch; existing line smoke sends empty events only ([test/README.md:48](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/test/README.md:48), [line-bot-smoke.yml:51](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/.github/workflows/line-bot-smoke.yml:51)). **Compare first replacement** - `runPromptPolisher` first: lower business/safety blast radius, simpler output contract, better first migration candidate. - `runRiskChecker` first: higher trust/compliance impact if regression, so riskier before call-site integration tests exist. **Recommendation** Replace `runPromptPolisher` first, via wrapper only (not orchestrator), with explicit `userError -> error` mapping and limit-alignment guard; keep `runRiskChecker` direct until index-level command-path tests are added.
